question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getHooks()] Can an unprivileged external user supply lowerBound=0 and upperBound=Long.MAX_VALUE in HooksRequest to cause findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween() to return every hook record for a given ownerId without any meaningful range restriction, resulting in mass data exposure of hook records that should be scoped to a narrower range?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getHooks()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a HooksRequest where lowerBound is greater than upperBound, causing the in-memory filter hookId >= lowerBound && hookId <= upperBound to always return an empty idsInRange list and silently suppress all hook records that should legitimately be returned, leading to missing records in the mirror node export?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getHooks()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a HooksRequest with a large hookIds list that spans the full Long range, bypassing the intent of range filtering and causing findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn() to return hook records outside the intended export window, producing incorrect records in the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getHooks()] Can an unprivileged external user manipulate the ownerId parameter in HooksRequest such that entityService.lookup() resolves to a different entity's numeric ID, causing findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween() to return hook records belonging to another owner and exposing incorrect records in the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getHooks()] Can an unprivileged external user set the limit field in HooksRequest to an arbitrarily large integer, causing PageRequest.of(0, limit, sort) to fetch an unbounded number of hook records in a single response, resulting in excessive and potentially incorrect records being exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getHooks()] Can an unprivileged external user toggle the Sort.Direction order in HooksRequest between ASC and DESC to change which page of hook records is returned by findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween(), causing different sets of records to be exported and leading to inconsistent or missing records in the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getHooks()] Can an unprivileged external user provide a hookIds list containing duplicate Long values, causing the in-memory filter to pass duplicates through to findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn(), which may return duplicate hook records or behave non-deterministically, resulting in incorrect records exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getHooks()] Can an unprivileged external user supply hookIds containing Long.MIN_VALUE or Long.MAX_VALUE boundary values, causing the range filter hookId >= lowerBound && hookId <= upperBound to behave unexpectedly due to long overflow, resulting in incorrect inclusion or exclusion of hook records in the mirror node export?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getHooks()] Can an unprivileged external user omit the hookIds list entirely (empty collection) while also omitting lowerBound and upperBound, causing findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween() to be called with bounds 0 and Long.MAX_VALUE and returning all hook records for the owner, leading to over-export of records to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getHookStorage()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a HookStorageRequest with an empty keys list and keyLowerBound=0x00...00 and keyUpperBound=0xFF...FF, causing findByOwnerIdAndHookIdAndKeyBetweenAndDeletedIsFalse() to return all non-deleted storage entries for a hook, resulting in mass exposure of hook storage records in the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getHookStorage()] Can an unprivileged external user set isHistorical() to true by providing any non-empty timestamp Bound, switching execution to getHookStorageChange() which uses a different query path that does NOT filter deleted=false the same way as the non-historical path, causing deleted hook storage records to appear in the mirror node export?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getHookStorage()] Can an unprivileged external user provide a HookStorageRequest with keys that are shorter than 32 bytes, relying on DomainUtils.leftPadBytes() padding behavior in getKeysInRange() to cause a key comparison mismatch between the in-memory filter and the database query, resulting in missing records in the mirror node export?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getHookStorage()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a HookStorageRequest where keyLowerBound equals keyUpperBound (a single-key range), causing findByOwnerIdAndHookIdAndKeyBetweenAndDeletedIsFalse() to return only that exact key's record, while the same key submitted via the keys list path would go through getKeysInRange() and potentially be filtered out, creating inconsistent record export behavior?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getHookStorage()] Can an unprivileged external user provide a HookStorageRequest with a non-empty keys list where all keys fall outside [keyLowerBound, keyUpperBound], causing getKeysInRange() to return an empty list and the service to return an empty result, silently suppressing records that exist in the database and causing missing records in the mirror node export?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getHookStorage()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that Arrays.compareUnsigned() is used in getKeysInRange() while the database may use signed byte comparison for the key column, causing a discrepancy where keys that pass the in-memory filter are not returned by the database query, resulting in missing records exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getHookStorage()] Can an unprivileged external user set the limit in HookStorageRequest to an extremely large value, causing PageRequest.of(0, limit, sort) to fetch an unbounded number of HookStorage records, resulting in excessive records being exported to the mirror node beyond what is intended?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getHookStorage()] Can an unprivileged external user provide a HookStorageRequest with a hookId that does not belong to the specified ownerId, and since the repository query filters by both ownerId and hookId, verify whether the service correctly rejects cross-owner hookId access or silently returns empty results, potentially masking incorrect record scoping in the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getHookStorageChange()] Can an unprivileged external user provide a timestamp Bound with timestampLowerBound=0 and timestampUpperBound=Long.MAX_VALUE, causing findByKeyBetweenAndTimestampBetween() to return all historical hook storage changes across all time, resulting in over-export of records to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getHookStorageChange()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the DISTINCT ON (key) clause in findByKeyBetweenAndTimestampBetween() which lacks an explicit ORDER BY on consensusTimestamp DESC, causing PostgreSQL to return a non-deterministic (not necessarily the latest) record per key, resulting in stale or incorrect hook storage records being exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getHookStorageChange()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the DISTINCT ON (key) clause in findByKeyInAndTimestampBetween() which also lacks an explicit ORDER BY on consensusTimestamp DESC, causing the query to return a non-deterministic historical record per key rather than the most recent change within the timestamp window, leading to incorrect records in the mirror node export?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getHookStorageChange()] Can an unprivileged external user observe that the native query in HookStorageChangeRepository hardcodes 0 as created_timestamp, causing all historical HookStorage records returned via getHookStorageChange() to have an incorrect createdTimestamp of 0 instead of the actual creation time, resulting in incorrect records exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getHookStorageChange()] Can an unprivileged external user provide a timestamp range that overlaps with a period when a hook storage key was deleted (value_written is null or empty), causing the historical query to return a record with deleted=true and value=null, which may be incorrectly interpreted as a valid record by the mirror node consumer?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getHookStorageChange()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a timestamp Bound where timestampLowerBound equals timestampUpperBound (a single nanosecond), causing findByKeyBetweenAndTimestampBetween() to return only changes at that exact consensus timestamp, potentially missing records that were written in the same block but at a different sub-nanosecond ordering, leading to missing records in the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getHookStorageChange()] Can an unprivileged external user provide a HookStorageRequest with both a non-empty keys list and a timestamp Bound, causing the service to call findByKeyInAndTimestampBetween() with the raw keys collection (not keysInRange), bypassing the in-memory range filter that getKeysInRange() applies, and returning records for keys outside the intended key range?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getHookStorageChange()] Can an unprivileged external user observe that getHookStorageChange() passes the raw keys collection (request.getKeys()) to findByKeyInAndTimestampBetween() rather than keysInRange, meaning keys outside [keyLowerBound, keyUpperBound] are still sent to the database query, causing records for out-of-range keys to be returned and exported incorrectly to the mirror node?
